top_areas: String concatenation with += in loop in processOrders

prioritized_issues.issue: N+1 database queries — query inside loop instead of batch query
prioritized_issues.explanation: A new SQL query is executed inside the loop for each order ID, creating N+1 round trips to the database. This massively increases network and database CPU consumption.
prioritized_issues.optimization: Use a single batch query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE id IN (...), then process results in memory.
prioritized_issues.estimated_savings: Could reduce network+DB CPU energy by ~90%

prioritized_issues.issue: String concatenation with += in loop (SQL building)
prioritized_issues.explanation: Using += for string concatenation in a loop creates a new String object each iteration, causing unnecessary memory allocations and garbage collection overhead.
prioritized_issues.snippet: sql += "SELECT * FROM orders WHERE id = " + orderIds.get(i) + ";";
prioritized_issues.optimization: Use StringBuilder or a parameterized batch query with prepared statement.
prioritized_issues.estimated_savings: Could reduce memory allocation energy by ~70%

prioritized_issues.issue: Reading file one byte at a time — extremely inefficient I/O
prioritized_issues.explanation: Using fis.read() to read one byte at a time causes a system call per byte. For a config file of even a few KB, this means thousands of expensive context switches.
prioritized_issues.optimization: Use BufferedReader with InputStreamReader, or read the entire file into a byte array with readAllBytes(). Also use StringBuilder for content accumulation.
prioritized_issues.estimated_savings: Could reduce disk I/O energy by ~90%

prioritized_issues.issue: O(n²) nested loop for duplicate detection
prioritized_issues.explanation: The nested loop compares every element with every other element, resulting in O(n²) comparisons. For large lists this is extremely CPU-intensive.
prioritized_issues.optimization: Use a HashSet to track seen elements. For each element, if it's already in the set, it's a duplicate. This reduces to O(n) time.
prioritized_issues.estimated_savings: Could reduce CPU energy by ~95% for large lists

prioritized_issues.issue: Connection created per method call instead of using connection pool
prioritized_issues.explanation: DriverManager.getConnection() creates a new database connection each time processOrders is called. Connection establishment is expensive (TCP handshake, SSL, auth).
prioritized_issues.optimization: Use a connection pool (HikariCP, DBCP2) or reuse a shared connection.
prioritized_issues.estimated_savings: Could reduce connection overhead energy by ~80% for repeated calls

recommendations: Replace loop with batch query (IN clause) in processOrders
Use StringBuilder for SQL building or parameterized queries
Replace byte-by-byte read with BufferedReader or readAllBytes in readConfig
Replace nested loop with HashSet for O(n) duplicate detection in findDuplicates
Use connection pooling (HikariCP) instead of DriverManager.getConnection()
